description: HP 8902A Measuring Receiver with HP-IB (HP-IB compatibility per IEEE 488-1978: SH1, AH1, T5, L4, SR1, RL1, PP0, DC1, DT1, C0). Programmed with a two-character alphameric code language (Tables 3-11/3-12, Function-to-Code and Code-to-Function summaries). Functions are entered either as a two-character code, as a number plus a units suffix, or as a special-function prefix (number followed by code SP) optionally with a number and suffix. Numeric data is a signed mantissa of up to eight digits with one decimal point and an optional one/two-digit signed exponent. The instrument is in one measurement mode at a time; modes, modifiers (detectors, filters, de-emphasis, ranges, ratio/log/lin) and the extensive Special Functions (number + SP) configure the measurement. Functions sharing the same leading letter must be sent in numeric order (e.g. P1 before P4); recommended program-string order is AU, frequency/units, measurement mode, detector, de-emphasis, filters, special functions, ratio, trigger. Measurement results are read back in fundamental units (Hz, watts, radians, dB, %) in a fixed HP-IB output format (signed mantissa, two status digits, signed exponent, CR LF). Errors are output to HP-IB as +9000000NNE+01 where the error code NN is recovered as (value - 9E10)/1000.

identity.description: Identify Instrument. Outputs an identifying number over HP-IB so a controller can distinguish between an HP 8901B and an HP 8902A. The instrument must be addressed to talk after sending ID to read the response.

commands.name: automaticOperation
commands.description: Automatic Operation. Returns the instrument to fully automatic operation; sets all special functions prefixed 1 through 10 to their zero-suffix mode and most other special functions to zero-suffix. Should appear at the beginning of a program string. (On HP 8901A the equivalent was A1.)

commands.name: instrumentPreset
commands.description: Instrument Preset. Resets the instrument to a known preset state (same effect as the HP-IB Device Clear, DCL).

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Clear (CLEAR key). Clears the current entry; for volatile measurement modes returns the instrument to the last selected non-volatile measurement mode. (On HP 8901A this was K1.)

commands.name: modeAm
commands.description: Select AM measurement mode (measures AM modulation depth in %).

commands.name: modeFm
commands.description: Select FM measurement mode (measures peak frequency deviation in Hz/kHz).

commands.name: modePm
commands.description: Select PM (Phase Modulation) measurement mode (measures peak phase deviation in radians).

commands.name: modeRfPower
commands.description: Select RF Power measurement mode (uses the power sensor). If no power sensor is connected, RF Level measurement (35.0SP) is initiated instead.

commands.name: modeFrequency
commands.description: Select RF Frequency measurement mode (measures the input signal frequency).

commands.name: modeAudioFrequency
commands.description: Select Audio Frequency measurement mode. Measures the frequency of an external audio signal at AUDIO INPUT, or of the internally demodulated signal when used with A1.

commands.name: modeAudioDistortion
commands.description: Select Audio Distortion measurement mode. Measures distortion of an external audio signal at AUDIO INPUT, or of the internally demodulated signal when used with A1.

commands.name: modeIfLevel
commands.description: Select IF Level measurement mode.

commands.name: modeTunedRfLevel
commands.description: Select Tuned RF Level measurement mode (synchronous and average detector based RF level).

commands.name: modeFrequencyError
commands.description: Select Frequency Error measurement mode. Displays the difference between the frequency entered via the keyboard (with MZ) and the actual RF input frequency.

commands.name: detectorPeakPlus
commands.description: Select PEAK+ detector for modulation measurements.

commands.name: detectorPeakMinus
commands.description: Select PEAK- detector for modulation measurements.

commands.name: detectorPeakHold
commands.description: Select PEAK HOLD detector.

commands.name: detectorAverage
commands.description: Select AVG (RMS Calibrated) average detector.

commands.name: detectorDist1kHz
commands.description: Select 1 kHz distortion detector (1 kHz DISTN).

commands.name: detectorDist400Hz
commands.description: Select 400 Hz distortion detector (400 Hz DISTN).

commands.name: detectorRms
commands.description: Select RMS detector.

commands.name: detectorPeakPlusMinusBy2
commands.description: Select PEAK +/-/2 detector (average of positive and negative peaks divided by 2).

commands.name: highPassFiltersOff
commands.description: High-Pass (HP) filters off.

commands.name: filter50Hz
commands.description: Turn on 50 Hz high-pass filter.

commands.name: filter300Hz
commands.description: Turn on 300 Hz high-pass filter.

commands.name: lowPassFiltersOff
commands.description: Low-Pass (LP) filters off.

commands.name: filter3kHz
commands.description: Turn on 3 kHz low-pass filter.

commands.name: filter15kHz
commands.description: Turn on 15 kHz low-pass filter.

commands.name: filterGreater20kHz
commands.description: Turn on >20 kHz low-pass filter.

commands.name: preDisplayAndDeEmphasisOff
commands.description: Pre-Display off and FM de-emphasis off. Turns off all FM de-emphasis (does not turn off pre-display if the Audio RMS Detector D8 is on).

commands.name: preDisplayAndDeEmphasisOn
commands.description: Pre-Display on and FM de-emphasis on.

commands.name: deEmphasis25us
commands.description: Select 25 microsecond FM de-emphasis.

commands.name: deEmphasis50us
commands.description: Select 50 microsecond FM de-emphasis.

commands.name: deEmphasis75us
commands.description: Select 75 microsecond FM de-emphasis.

commands.name: deEmphasis750us
commands.description: Select 750 microsecond FM de-emphasis (divide-by-10 on FM ranges when also using PRE-DISPLAY).

commands.name: displayLog
commands.description: Display result in logarithmic (dB) units. With R1 selected this gives a dB ratio.

commands.name: displayLinear
commands.description: Display result in linear units. With R1 selected this gives a % ratio.

commands.name: ratioOff
commands.description: RATIO off.

commands.name: ratioOn
commands.description: RATIO on. The current measurement is set as the ratio reference; subsequent readings are relative to it. Combine with LN for % ratio or LG for dB ratio.

commands.name: previousRatio
commands.description: PREVIOUS RATIO. Restore/use the previous ratio reference (see also Special Function 11.2).

commands.name: extAttenOff
commands.description: dB External Attenuation off (do not apply the external attenuation/gain offset).

commands.name: extAttenOn
commands.description: dB External Attenuation on (apply the external attenuation/gain offset set with the dB EXT ATTEN key / Special Function 25).

commands.name: calibrateOff
commands.description: CALIBRATE off.

commands.name: calibrateOn
commands.description: CALIBRATE on. Initiates the calibration sequence for the active measurement (AM/FM calibrator, Tuned RF Level range calibration, etc.).

commands.name: calFactorPercent
commands.description: % Calibration Factor. With no number, reads the current calibration factor; preceded by a number, enters a calibration factor in percent.

commands.name: setReference
commands.description: SET REF. Set the present measurement as the reference (e.g. for relative Tuned RF Level / attenuator measurements; see Special Function 26).

commands.name: zero
commands.description: ZERO. Zeroes the RF power sensor for RF Power measurements. Ensure zero power at the SENSOR input before zeroing.

commands.name: saveCal
commands.description: SAVE CAL. Saves/enables the current calibration factor.

commands.name: autoTuning
commands.description: AUTO TUNING. The Measuring Receiver automatically tunes to the input signal.

commands.name: inputFrequencyHz
commands.description: Input Frequency entry in Hz. Precede with a numeric value to manually set the input (tune) frequency in hertz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: signed mantissa up to 8 digits with optional exponent

commands.name: inputFrequencyMHz
commands.description: MHz (Input Frequency) entry. With a preceding number, manually tunes to that frequency in MHz; with no number, centers the IF frequency. After Clear or power-up (with MZ active) the receiver tunes to 100 MHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: signed mantissa up to 8 digits with optional exponent

commands.name: incrementUp
commands.description: Increment input frequency up (up-arrow Hz). Steps the tuned frequency up by the current increment.

commands.name: incrementDown
commands.description: Increment input frequency down (down-arrow Hz). Steps the tuned frequency down by the current increment.

commands.name: incrementUpKHz
commands.description: Increment up by kHz (up-arrow kHz). Steps the tuned frequency up in kHz steps. (On HP 8901A this was F2.)

commands.name: incrementDownKHz
commands.description: Increment down by kHz (down-arrow kHz). Steps the tuned frequency down in kHz steps. (On HP 8901A this was F3.)

commands.name: displayFrequency
commands.description: DISPLAY FREQ. Display the tuned/input frequency.

commands.name: displayIncrement
commands.description: DISPLAY INCREMENT. Display the current frequency increment value used by the increment keys.

commands.name: trackModeOff
commands.description: TRACK Mode off (lock mode). Uses the low-noise lock mode for tuning.

commands.name: trackModeOn
commands.description: TRACK Mode on. The receiver continuously tracks the input frequency (useful for drifting signals).

commands.name: store
commands.description: STORE. Store the current instrument settings into a register; precede/follow with a register number per the Store/Recall procedure.

commands.name: recall
commands.description: RECALL. Recall instrument settings from a storage register.

commands.name: autoRangeOn
commands.description: Auto-Ranging on (RANGE HOLD off).

commands.name: rangeHold
commands.description: RANGE HOLD (holds the current range; disables auto-ranging).

commands.name: enableErrors
commands.description: ENABLE ERRORS. Enable error message reporting.

commands.name: disableErrors
commands.description: DISABLE ERRORS. Disable error messages (same as Special Function 8.7 for Errors 01-04).

commands.name: modulationOutput
commands.description: MODULATION OUTPUT. Routes the demodulated signal to the MODULATION OUTPUT connector.

commands.name: audioInput
commands.description: AUDIO INPUT. Selects internal audio measurements (uses the Receiver's demodulated signal) for Audio Frequency/Distortion/SINAD. (On HP 8901A this was A1 or AU.)

commands.name: externalAudio
commands.description: Select external audio measurements applied at the AUDIO INPUT connector (used with S1, S2, SINAD, etc.).

commands.name: triggerOff
commands.description: Trigger Off.

commands.name: hold
commands.description: Hold. Holds the measurement (one measurement is held, awaiting a trigger).

commands.name: triggerImmediate
commands.description: Trigger Immediate. Takes one measurement with no settling delay, then holds.

commands.name: triggerWithSettling
commands.description: Trigger with Settling. Takes one measurement after a settling-time delay, then holds.

commands.name: unitsMillivolt
commands.description: mV units. Specifies millivolts as the units suffix for a preceding numeric entry.

commands.name: unitsMicrovolt
commands.description: microvolt (uV) units. Specifies microvolts as the units suffix for a preceding numeric entry.

commands.name: unitsVolt
commands.description: V units. Specifies volts as the units suffix for a preceding numeric entry.

commands.name: unitsWatt
commands.description: W units. Specifies watts as the units suffix for a preceding numeric entry.

commands.name: hexA
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit A (used when entering hexadecimal data).

commands.name: hexB
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit B.

commands.name: hexC
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit C.

commands.name: hexD
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit D.

commands.name: hexE
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit E.

commands.name: hexF
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit F.

commands.name: specialFunction
commands.description: SPECIAL FUNCTION terminator. Completes entry of a special-function code: send the special-function number (e.g. 4.4) followed by SP. Special functions configure detectors, ranges, gains, resolution, calibration factors, status, etc. See the numbered specialFunction* commands.

commands.name: specialFunctionDisplayOperator
commands.description: SPECIAL, SPECIAL (SS). Display instrument-selected special functions (the special functions the instrument has currently selected).

commands.name: specialFunction1RfInputAttenuationGain
commands.description: Special Function 1 - RF Input Attenuation and Gain. 1.0 automatic selection; 1.1=0 dB atten, 1.2=10 dB, 1.3=20 dB, 1.4=30 dB, 1.5=40 dB, 1.6=50 dB attenuation; 1.7=24 dB gain, 1.8=14 dB gain; 1.9 automatic selection (10 dB attenuator always inserted, Tuned RF Level only; raises Range 3 by 10 dB).

commands.name: specialFunction2AudioRange
commands.description: Special Function 2 - Audio Range. 2.0 automatic; 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 select progressively higher fixed audio ranges (AM %/pk, FM kHz/pk, PM rad/pk) depending on detector.

commands.name: specialFunction3RfIfFilters
commands.description: Special Function 3 - RF and IF Filters / IF bandwidth selection. 3.0 automatic. With RF high-pass filter out: 3.6=455 kHz wide (2.5 MHz BW), 3.5=455 kHz narrow (30 kHz BW), 3.1=1.5 MHz wide (200 kHz BW), 3.2=1.5 MHz (2.5 MHz, narrow filter out). With RF high-pass filter in: 3.8, 3.7, 3.3, 3.4 select the corresponding configurations.

commands.name: specialFunction4TunedRfLevelDetector
commands.description: Special Function 4 - Tuned RF Level Detector and Averaging. 4.0 synchronous detector/auto averaging; 4.1=synch/10s; 4.2=synch/1s; 4.3=synch/0.1s; 4.4 average detector/auto; 4.5=avg/10s; 4.6=avg/1s; 4.7=avg/0.1s. Use the average detector (4.4-4.7) for noisy/drifting signals.

commands.name: specialFunction5AudioDetectorResponse
commands.description: Special Function 5 - Audio Detector Response. 5.0 fast response time; 5.1 slow response time. Helps stabilize modulation or SINAD measurements on unstable/noisy signals.

commands.name: specialFunction6Alc
commands.description: Special Function 6 - Automatic Level Control. 6.0 slow ALC (AM rates > 20 Hz); 6.1 fast ALC (AM rates > 1 kHz); 6.2 ALC off.

commands.name: specialFunction7FrequencyResolution
commands.description: Special Function 7 - RF Frequency Resolution. 7.0 automatic; 7.1=10 Hz; 7.2=100 Hz; 7.3=1 kHz; 7.4 fine resolution (0.1 Hz for signals <10.1 MHz, 1 Hz from 10.1-1300 MHz; RF frequency measurements only).

commands.name: specialFunction8ErrorMessageControl
commands.description: Special Function 8 - Error Message Disable/Enable Control. 8.0 automatic; 8.1 disable Error 01; 8.2 disable Errors 02,03; 8.3 disable 01,02,03; 8.4 disable 04; 8.5 disable 01,04; 8.6 disable 02,03,04; 8.7 disable 01-04 (same as DISABLE ERROR / B1); 8.8 enable 01-04.

commands.name: specialFunction9IfGain
commands.description: Special Function 9 - IF Gain (10 dB steps, 0 to 60 dB). 9.0 automatic (Tuned RF Level); 9.1=0 dB; 9.2=10 dB; 9.3=20 dB; 9.4=30 dB; 9.5=40 dB; 9.6=50 dB; 9.7=60 dB.

commands.name: specialFunction10RfPowerRange
commands.description: Special Function 10 - RF Power Range. 10.0 automatic; 10.1 Range #1 (most sensitive); 10.2 #2; 10.3 #3; 10.4 #4; 10.5 Range #5 (least sensitive).

commands.name: specialFunction11PreviousRatio
commands.description: Special Function 11 - Previous Ratio. 11.2 display the reference set with the RATIO key.

commands.name: specialFunction12FmCalibration
commands.description: Special Function 12 - FM Calibration for another HP 8902/8901 family analyzer. 12.0 display computed peak FM; 12.1 display demodulated peak residual FM; 12.2 display demodulated peak FM.

commands.name: specialFunction13AmCalibration
commands.description: Special Function 13 - AM Calibration for another HP 8902/8901 family analyzer. 13.0 display computed peak AM; 13.1 display demodulated peak residual AM; 13.2 display demodulated peak AM.

commands.name: specialFunction14MeasurementLimits
commands.description: Special Function 14 - Measurement Limits (used with the RATIO key). 14.0 clear limits/LIMIT off; 14.1 set lower limit to ratio reference; 14.2 set upper limit; 14.3 restore lower limit; 14.4 restore upper limit; 14.5 display lower limit; 14.6 display upper limit; 14.7 display lower-limit measurement code; 14.8 display upper-limit measurement code; 14.9 display limit status (Lower.Upper, 0=disabled, 1=enabled).

commands.name: specialFunction15TimeBaseCheck
commands.description: Special Function 15 - Time Base Check. 15.0 display whether the Option 002 oven is warm (0=warm; Error 12 if cold); 15.1 display whether internal or external time base is in use (0=internal, 1=external).

commands.name: specialFunction16AmCalFactor
commands.description: Special Function 16 - AM Calibration Factor. 16.0 disable AM cal factor; 16.1 enable; 16.2 display AM cal factor (displays 0 if not enabled).

commands.name: specialFunction17FmCalFactor
commands.description: Special Function 17 - FM Calibration Factor. 17.0 disable FM cal factor; 17.1 enable; 17.2 display FM cal factor (displays 0 if not enabled).

commands.name: specialFunction18ToneBurstReceiver
commands.description: Special Function 18 - Tone-Burst Receiver. Configures the instrument as a tone-burst receiver; a delay of NN ms (1 to 99) is inserted between carrier detection and unsquelching of MODULATION OUTPUT/AUDIO INPUT. If NN is 0, the delay is 99 ms.

commands.name: specialFunction21HpibAddress
commands.description: Special Function 21 - HP-IB Address. 21.0 display the HP-IB address in the form AAAAAAA.TLS where AAAAAAA = binary address, T=1=talk only, L=1=listen only, S=1=requesting service.

commands.name: specialFunction22ServiceRequestMask
commands.description: Special Function 22 - Service Request (SRQ) Mask. NN (0-63) is the sum of weighted conditions: 1=Data Ready, 2=HP-IB error (cannot be disabled), 4=Instrument error, 8=Limit exceeded, 16=Frequency Offset mode state change, 32=Recal or Uncal. 22.64SP reads back (displays) the current SRQ mask.

commands.name: specialFunction23ExternalLo
commands.description: Special Function 23 - External LO (Option 030). 23.0 return LO to internal; 23.1 set LO to external; 23.2 display LO status (0=internal, 1=external).

commands.name: specialFunction24SelectivePowerMeasurement
commands.description: Special Function 24 - Selective Power Measurement (Option 030). 24.0 enter selective power mode; 24.1 wide-BW IF filter (displays IF level in volts); 24.2 set 24.1 level as 0 dB ref; 24.3 narrower-BW IF filter (displays IF level); 24.4 set 24.3 level as 0 dB ref; 24.5 narrowest 2.5 kHz IF filter for SSB noise (displays IF level); 24.6 set 24.5 level as 0 dB ref; 24.7 display noise normalized to 1 Hz BW; 24.8 enable setting noise BW value; 24.9 display noise BW value.

commands.name: specialFunction25ExternalAttenuation
commands.description: Special Function 25 - External Attenuation. 25.2 display the current external attenuation offset in dB (amplification shown as negative attenuation). Used with dB EXT ATTEN (N1) to scale level measurements.

commands.name: specialFunction26RelativeTunedRfLevel
commands.description: Special Function 26 - Relative Tuned RF Level (used with SET REF). 26.0 exit relative mode and enter absolute Tuned RF Level mode; 26.1 re-enter relative mode using the previous SET REF reference.

commands.name: specialFunction27FrequencyOffsetControl
commands.description: Special Function 27 - Frequency Offset Control (down-converted signals with external mixer/LO). 27.0 exit Frequency Offset mode; 27.1 enter Frequency Offset mode; 27.2 display external LO frequency; 27.3 prepare to enter/enable the LO frequency (follow 27.3SP with the LO frequency in MHz, 0 to 40700 MHz, then MZ).

commands.name: specialFunction29Sinad
commands.description: Special Function 29.0 - SINAD Measurement. Make SINAD (Signal-to-Noise-And-Distortion) measurements on internally demodulated signals or externally applied audio (use with A2 for external).

commands.name: specialFunction30ExternalAudioRmsLevel
commands.description: Special Function 30 - External Audio RMS Level. 30.0 measure the true RMS level of an audio signal applied to the AUDIO INPUT connector.

commands.name: specialFunction31TunedRfLevelNoiseCorrection
commands.description: Special Function 31 - Tuned RF Level Noise Correction (used with the IF average detector, 4.4-4.7, on low-level signals). 31.0 turn off noise correction; 31.1 turn on (enter once before making level measurements); 31.2 display status (1=off, 0=on). Maintained through power down.

commands.name: specialFunction32Resolution
commands.description: Special Function 32 - dB and Frequency Resolution. 32.0 normal resolution (power 0.01 dB; audio 100-250 kHz at 1 Hz); 32.1 increased resolution (power 0.001 dB; audio 100-250 kHz at 0.1 Hz); 32.2 display resolution status (0=normal, 1=increased); 32.9 Tuned RF Level using track-mode tuning for low-level drifting signals (equivalent to 4.4SP, 8.1SP, log units, track mode and 27.3SP). 32.1 and 32.9 can be enabled simultaneously.

commands.name: specialFunction33LoFrequency
commands.description: Special Function 33.0 - LO Frequency Measurement. Display the LO frequency for the signal being measured.

commands.name: specialFunction34IfFrequency
commands.description: Special Function 34.0 - IF Frequency Measurement. Display the IF frequency for the signal being measured.

commands.name: specialFunction35RfLevel
commands.description: Special Function 35.0 - RF Level Measurement (RF peak detector). Measures peak RF power; selected automatically when RF POWER is chosen with no power sensor connected. (On HP 8901A this measurement was M4.)

commands.name: specialFunction36PeakTunedRfLevel
commands.description: Special Function 36.0 - Peak Tuned RF Level Measurement (IF peak detector). Less accurate/sensitive than Tuned RF Level; use for drifting frequencies or flatness vs carrier frequency. (On HP 8901A this measurement was S4.)

commands.name: specialFunction37RfPowerCalFactors
commands.description: Special Function 37 - RF Power Calibration Factors (two tables; active table depends on Frequency Offset mode state). 37.0 automatic cal factors; 37.1 manual cal factors; 37.2 display status (0=auto, 1=manual/held); 37.3 enter frequency then cal factor; 37.4 display table size; 37.5 prepare to recall the reference cal factor; 37.6 prepare to recall next frequency/cal-factor pair; 37.9 clear the table.

commands.name: specialFunction38DisplayTunedRfLevelCalFactors
commands.description: Special Function 38 - Display Tuned RF Level Calibration Factors. 38.1 display cal factor for Range #1; 38.2 Range #2; 38.3 Range #3; 38.4 display SET REF reference cal value.

commands.name: specialFunction39EnterTunedRfLevelCalFactors
commands.description: Special Function 39 - Enter Tuned RF Level Calibration Factors. 39.1 enter cal factor for Range #1; 39.2 Range #2; 39.3 Range #3; 39.4 enter SET REF reference cal value; 39.9 clear all calibration factors.

commands.name: specialFunction42SoftwareDateCode
commands.description: Special Function 42 - Software/Firmware Date Code. 42.0 display the firmware date code.
